scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT

AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 2024

RAM KUMAR,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

SURESH PATEL,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF RS. 15,00,000/- WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

UNDER SECTION 26 OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, 1908

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the Defendant resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru, which falls within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and the cause of action arose within such territorial limits as the loan transaction was entered into and the amount was transferred at Bengaluru within the jurisdiction of this Court.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits of this nature.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. The Plaintiff is a resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

5. The Defendant is a resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru.

6. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a hand loan of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) to the Defendant.

7. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff transferred a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) to the Defendant through NEFT bank transfer, the transaction being evidenced by UTR: AXIB20240315123456 (a true copy of the NEFT transfer details/bank statement showing the said transaction is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure A).

8. There was no written agreement executed between the parties in respect of the said loan transaction. However, the Defendant acknowledged the loan through WhatsApp messages sent to the Plaintiff (a true printout of the WhatsApp messages acknowledging the loan is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure B).

9. The Defendant was obligated to repay the loan amount to the Plaintiff upon demand. The Plaintiff performed his part of the obligation by advancing the loan amount to the Defendant in full.

10. Despite the Plaintiff having duly advanced the loan amount, the Defendant failed to repay the same. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands to the Defendant for repayment of the loan amount.

11. The Defendant committed default in repayment of the loan amount on or about 20.06.2024, when the Defendant failed and refused to repay the amount despite demand, thereby giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to claim interest on the outstanding amount.

12. Left with no alternative, the Plaintiff, through his advocate, caused a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 to be served upon the Defendant, calling upon the Defendant to repay the loan amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- together with interest within the stipulated period (a true copy of the legal notice along with the postal receipt and acknowledgment card is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure C).

13. Despite receipt of the legal notice, the Defendant has failed and refused to repay the loan amount or respond satisfactorily to the notice.

14. As a direct and proximate consequence of the Defendant's failure to repay the loan, the Plaintiff has suffered loss and is deprived of the use of his funds. The Plaintiff has no alternative but to approach this Hon'ble Court for the reliefs sought herein.

LEGAL BASIS

15. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is liable under {{CONTRACT_ACT_PROVISION}} inasmuch as the Plaintiff advanced the loan amount to the Defendant without any intention to do so gratuitously, and the Defendant has enjoyed the benefit of the said amount, and is therefore bound to compensate the Plaintiff by repaying the principal amount together with interest.

16. Without prejudice to the above and in the alternative, the Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is also liable under {{CONTRACT_ACT_PROVISION_ALT}} for restitution. The Defendant having received the amount is bound to restore the same to the Plaintiff.

17. The Plaintiff further submits that the Defendant is liable to pay damages under {{CONTRACT_ACT_PROVISION_DAMAGES}}, which provides that when a contract has been broken, the party who suffers by such breach is entitled to receive compensation for any loss or damage caused thereby. The Plaintiff is entitled to interest on the outstanding amount as damages for being deprived of the use of his capital.

18. It is submitted that no part performance was rendered by the Defendant in respect of the loan, and no set-off or counter-cla
